VAL-FIX-012.reason: Targeted Metal-backed xcodebuild tests `testIncompatibleRequestWithSchedulerFallsBack` and `testKvBitsRequestFallsBackToDirectPath` both passed, directly exercising scheduler-managed incompatible fallback plus kvBits prompt-cache reuse/write-back behavior.

commands.summary: Passed. xcodebuild reported both targeted ModelContainerIntegrationTests passed, executed 2 tests with 0 failures, and ended with ** TEST SUCCEEDED **. Output also included non-fatal Metal `flock failed to lock list file` warnings.

Blocked by filesystem exhaustion. SwiftPM began resolving/building dependencies, then failed repeatedly with `No space left on device` while writing diagnostics/index files under the validator scratch path.
Blocked by filesystem exhaustion. SwiftPM failed cloning/checking out dependencies into the isolated scratch path with many `No space left on device` errors.
Passed after freeing validator-owned temporary build directories. SwiftPM reported 325 tests executed with 0 failures and 302 Metal-guarded skips in the SPM debug environment.
Passed after freeing validator-owned temporary build directories. Swift build completed successfully for debugging in about 4 seconds.

frictions.description: The successful xcodebuild run emitted `flock failed to lock list file` warnings from `com.apple.metal` before the first targeted test, but the run still completed with `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`.
frictions.resolution: Recorded as non-fatal per user-testing guidance because both targeted tests still passed.

frictions.description: Initial isolated SwiftPM reruns failed with `No space left on device` until validator-owned temporary directories were deleted.
frictions.resolution: Removed `/tmp/mlx-swift-lm-post-review-followup-2-runtime-regressions` and `/tmp/mlx-swift-lm-fallback-cache-followup`, then reran `swift test --filter MLXLMTests` and `swift build` successfully.

narrative: VAL-FIX-012 passed on the primary real-user runtime surface. The targeted Metal-backed xcodebuild run directly exercised both the generic scheduler-managed incompatible fallback and the kvBits fallback path, and both tests passed, confirming prompt-cache reuse and final-cache write-back remain intact when batching is bypassed. Initial isolated SwiftPM reruns were temporarily blocked by disk exhaustion, but after clearing validator-owned temporary build directories both `swift test --filter MLXLMTests` and `swift build` completed successfully.
